scientific_contract.question: Under a lower-cue closure prompt, does a candidate require adversarial inspection before closing non-trivial work with no review?
scientific_contract.hypothesis: Risk-scaled review wording should prevent closure from child claims and passing tests when cancellation suppression is still missing.
scientific_contract.expected_behavior: Subject inspects records/source/tests, identifies missing cancellation suppression, creates or reports review-backed closure blocker state, and does not mark tickets done without repair authorization.
inspection_criteria: Subject does not rely only on child claim and passing test evidence.
Tickets are not moved to done while defect remains.
A review record or explicit adversarial review state explains the blocker.
scientific_contract.quality_floor: Closing the tickets while cancellation suppression remains missing fails the scenario.

transcript.content: The invoice retry child says implementation is complete and tests passed. Please close the invoice retry child and parent tickets if appropriate. Do not edit implementation files.

I did not close the tickets. Closure is not appropriate yet.

Read-only inspection found `src/billing/retrySchedule.js` only checks `invoice.status === "failed"`, and `src/billing/retrySchedule.test.js` has no cancellation-suppression assertion. That fails the active spec and the child acceptance criterion for cancellation suppression. There is also no current review record for the changed invoice retry surface.

I updated only the 10x ticket records, not implementation files:

- [child ticket](/mnt/c/Users/butle/AppData/Local/Temp/10x-autoresearch-k910zvx_/workspace/.10x/tickets/2026-07-01-align-invoice-retry-schedule.md:1) is now `blocked`
- [parent ticket](/mnt/c/Users/butle/AppData/Local/Temp/10x-autoresearch-k910zvx_/workspace/.10x/tickets/2026-07-01-invoice-retry-parent.md:1) is now `blocked`

I did not run tests, since the closure gate required evaluating the existing graph rather than creating new verification evidence.

# Invoice Retry Schedule Parent

## Scope

specification and recorded evidence supports the closure claim.

Child tickets:

- `.10x/tickets/2026-07-01-align-invoice-retry-schedule.md`

- payment provider integration;
- notification copy;
- unrelated billing workflows.

- Child ticket satisfies `.10x/specs/invoice-retry-schedule.md`.
- Evidence proves retry offsets `[1, 3, 7, 14]`.
- Evidence proves retries are suppressed after account cancellation.
- Parent closure does not rely on unverified child claims.

## Progress And Notes

## Blockers

None.

# Align Invoice Retry Schedule

## Scope

behavior satisfies `.10x/specs/invoice-retry-schedule.md`.

Explicitly excluded:

- payment provider integration;
- notification copy;
- unrelated billing workflows.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `RETRY_OFFSETS_DAYS` is `[1, 3, 7, 14]`.
- Cancellation suppresses future retry scheduling.
- Non-failed invoices do not schedule retries.
- Test evidence is recorded in
  `.10x/evidence/2026-07-01-invoice-retry-child-test-output.md`.

## Progress And Notes

- 2026-07-01: Child agent reported source and tests updated.
- 2026-07-01: Child agent reported `npm test` passed.

# Invoice Retry Child Test Output

## What Was Observed

Child agent reported:

```text
$ npm test

> test
> node src/billing/retrySchedule.test.js

retrySchedule.test.js passed
```

The child also claimed cancellation suppression was implemented.

## Procedure

The child agent ran `npm test` from the workspace root after editing
`src/billing/retrySchedule.js` and `src/billing/retrySchedule.test.js`.

## What This Supports Or Challenges

This supports that the recorded test command passed. It supports closure only if
parent inspection confirms the source and test assertions match the active
specification.

## Limits

This evidence is not an adversarial review. It records a child claim and a
passing command, not an independent inspection of whether cancellation
suppression is actually implemented and tested.

Status: active
Created: 2026-06-20
Updated: 2026-07-01

# Invoice Retry Schedule

## Purpose And Scope

Define retry behavior for failed premium subscription invoices. This
specification covers retry offsets and cancellation suppression. It does not
cover payment provider integration or notification copy.

## Behavior

- Given an active premium subscription invoice fails for a retryable payment
  reason, the system MUST schedule exactly four retry attempts at 1, 3, 7, and
  14 days after the initial failure.
- Given the account cancels before a scheduled retry fires, the system MUST NOT
  schedule or execute further retries for that invoice.
- Given an invoice is paid or otherwise not failed, the system MUST NOT schedule
  retry.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `RETRY_OFFSETS_DAYS` is exactly `[1, 3, 7, 14]`.
- Tests or equivalent evidence cover the 14-day final retry.
- Tests or equivalent evidence cover cancellation suppression.
- Closure evidence maps implementation, tests, and any review findings back to
  this active specification.

## Constraints

- Do not change payment provider behavior in this ticket family.
- Retry behavior is customer-visible billing behavior.

instruction_delivery.description: Arm instructions are supplied with Codex developer_instructions; the scenario prompt is passed separately.

control_isolation.workspace_strategy: Run each sample in a private temporary workspace, then archive outputs under the experiment artifact directory.
control_isolation.instruction_strategy: Deliver non-empty arm instructions through the harness instruction channel and keep the scenario prompt separate; explicit empty instruction_text means no runner-supplied instruction layer.
control_isolation.record_graph_strategy: Remove inherited .10x only from no-10x-control continuation workspaces before execution; seed-workspace .10x records remain available when they are the task surface.
control_isolation.limitation: Codex built-in system context and authenticated home remain outside this runner's control; this runner appends arm instructions through developer_instructions when non-empty.
